rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND M. AKASHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No petition for final distribution or third report of status of administration with a request for additional time has been filed yet, as required by Probate Code Section 12200 and as ordered on 5/12/25. Additionally, no I&A has been filed yet. [Prob. C. Sect. 8800(b).] The probate referee appointed on 1/4/23 was apparently never given the information about estate assets in over two years. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE PICHOTTO PROPERTY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 24,1993
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Ascertain Beneficiaries Of Trust, To Compel Trustee To Account, To Remove Trustee; And For An Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/20/2025, pending disposition of demurrer. Notice of the AMENDED petition must be given to each person entitled to notice, including Shawn Crane's personal representative. If there is no personal representative, notice must be given to each heir of Shawn Crane and each devisee and executor named in his will. See Probate Code section 17203(c). A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the next hearing. Direct notice must be given to each beneficiary represented by counsel, absent a directive signed by the beneficiary stating that notice given to counsel is sufficient. See CRC 7.51(a) & (b).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE PICHOTTO PROPERTY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 24,1993
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Return Of Trust Property, Financial Elder Abuse; Removal And Appointment Of Independednt Trustee; Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee, Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/20/2025, pending disposition of demurrer to the related petition to ascertain beneficiaries. Notice of the AMENDED petition must be given to each person entitled to notice, including Shawn Crane's personal representative. If there is no personal representative, notice must be given to each heir of Shawn Crane and each devisee and executor named in his will. See Probate Code section 17203(c). A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the next hearing. Direct notice must be given to each beneficiary represented by counsel, absent a directive signed by the beneficiary stating that notice given to counsel is sufficient. See CRC 7.51(a) & (b). In addition, the following issues have not been addressed since the 06/05/2024 hearing: 1) No declaration required by CCP section 377.32 has been filed by petitioner to whom the right to prosecute Leona May's elder abuse cause of action is alleged to have passed under Welfare & Institutions Code section 15657.3(d). 2) No DE-115 form is on file as to relief under section 850 against Shawn Crane's estate. If there is no personal representative, each successor must be served in the manner required by section 851(a)(2).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NEIL W. MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (4) The initial Petition for Letters of Administration, filed on 11/7/24, did not identify the decedent's parents and set forth their dates of death, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.3 and 14.18 D. This must be done now for the record, via a supplement, signed and verified by the administrator. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/13/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PASQUALINA ANGELINA BROWN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration must be filed, setting for the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C.1.] (4) Who is the trustee of the devisee trust? (5) If the trustee is the petitioner, all beneficiaries of the trust must be listed in item 8 of the petition, clearly identified as such, and must receive notice. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/13/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAOLO POLLEDRI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status hearing on filing either a petition for order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code 12200 et seq. will be set for 3/1/27. No appearance will be necessary if either petition is filed two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEBORAH JANE LINTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) The Amended Notice that was reportedly mailed on 7/23/25 was incomplete at item 1. (3) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Who was or were the deceased siblings and when did he/she/they die? (4) Item 3g(2)(c) of the petition should have been checked, as the petitioner is related to the decedent. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
